question: How much does a back-to-back cost a WNBA team, and do stars get load-managed on the second night?

definitions.b2b: 0 days off — a team playing the very next calendar day (derived rest = 1 day between games).
definitions.rest: days between a team's consecutive games in a season, derived from the game schedule (authoritative).
definitions.note: the stored per-game rest column is intentionally NOT used: 2023-26 only + internally inconsistent (backfill artifact). Rest is recomputed from the schedule.

headline.one_liner: A back-to-back costs a WNBA team about 1.0 points and 3 percentage points of win probability once you adjust for the fact that b2b teams are usually the road team — a real but modest edge.

player_fatigue.finding: No box-visible fatigue: minutes, points and TS% are ~flat b2b vs rested, even for stars.

caveats: B2B is rare in the WNBA (n=183 team-games over 11 seasons) — treat the b2b bucket as a gross estimate, not a precise per-team number.
Confound handled: b2b teams skew road (63% of b2b games). Raw margin overstates the pure b2b cost; the court-adjusted number is the honest one.
Rest-differential (home rest - away rest) is noisy and non-monotone — confounded by road-trip/schedule structure. Shown but flagged; the b2b bucket is the reliable read.
Team-strength confound not removed: rest state is quasi-random across a season so bias is small, but a single b2b bucket does not adjust for opponent quality.
Player age x b2b interaction NOT built: the box-score data carries no clean age field and b2b star-games (n=423) are too thin to split by age — omitted rather than invented.
6 legacy team abbreviations in the box scores do not map to the game results (relocations/renames) and drop from the player join — negligible coverage loss.
